name: Benchmarking & Microbenchmarking
def: Systematically measuring performance of code under controlled conditions.
problem: Quantify performance and detect regressions with statistical rigour, not guesswork.
mechanism: A harness runs warm-up then many timed iterations, controls noise, and reports distributions; microbenchmarks isolate a single operation (carefully, to avoid dead-code elimination).
failures: Compiler optimising away the measured work
Drawing system conclusions from micro numbers
whenNot: Before there is a measured perf concern
myths: "This microbenchmark proves X is faster" — micro results rarely predict real-system performance
